title: campanula rotundifolia subsp. ruscinonensis
intro: Campanula rotundifolia subsp. ruscinonensis, also known as the Ruscin bellflower, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the genus Campanula. It is primarily used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to treat various health issues. The Ruscin bellflower is believed to have several health benefits, including the ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of the Ruscin bellflower often involve infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of ailments, from respiratory problems to skin conditions.
